north_star: viggomeesters.com is a calm, public, proof-first map of Viggo’s work: visitors should understand the projects, systems, methods, and current operating style without needing private context.
wedge: Static pages with strong internal navigation, source-backed claims, local validation, and repo-local .go state so both humans and agents can maintain the site from a fresh clone.
target_user: Technical visitors, collaborators, recruiters, and maintenance agents working from the public repository.
core_promise: Every public page should answer three questions quickly: what is this, why does it matter, and what proof or next route exists? The repo should prove the same through scripts/check.sh, repo-complete, and .go readback.
current_truths: The site is intentionally static: no framework, no dependency install path, no hidden build output.
The repository is public and should stay public-safe by default.
Local-only validation is canonical while GitHub Actions is unavailable at account level.
SEO audit output is now the strongest visible improvement backlog: short titles/descriptions and duplicate descriptions dominate the issue count.
product_principles: one clear navigation shelf per intent
non_goals: no production deploy unless explicitly requested
no private vault data or raw agent runbooks in public pages
no broad rewrite of all pages in one pass
success_metrics: scripts/check.sh passes from a fresh clone
repo_complete public validate returns JA with zero hard blockers
go-workflow-stack validate/readback passes and reports repo-local mode
SEO baseline issue count trends down through bounded, page-cluster tasks
new public pages update canonical URL, sitemap, and local checks together
near_term_focus: Use .go tasks for bounded site improvements instead of chat-only intent.
Reduce high-confidence SEO metadata issues in page clusters.
Keep public readiness and local validation green after each slice.
